# Model predictions from fitted `fit_ageclass` objects

Model predictions from fitted `fit_ageclass` objects.

## Usage

``` r
# S3 method for class 's4t_ageclass_model'
predict(object, newdata, type = c("prob", "class", "cum.prob"), ...)
```

## Arguments

- object:

  a `s4t_ageclass_model` object

- newdata:

  A data frame object in the same format as aux_age_df (see
  documentation for `s4t_ch`) containing data to predict age-class. If
  omitted, the fitted values for the `aux_age_df` data in the s4t_ch
  from the fitted model object are returned.

- type:

  The prediction type. A `character` of one of the three option `prob`,
  `class`, or `cum.prob`.

- ...:

  not used (needed for generic consistency)

## Value

Depends on the type. `type = "prob"` returns a matrix of the
probabilities, `type = "class"` returns a vector of the mostly likely
age-class (highest probability), `type = "cum.prob"` returns the
cumulative probabilities as a matrix (probability that the individual is
at least a particular age-class).

## Examples

``` r
sim.dat <- sim_simple_s4t_ch(N = 2000)
m1 <- fit_ageclass(age_formula = ~ FL,
                   s4t_ch = sim.dat$s4t_ch)
predict(m1,type = "prob")
#>                 Age1       Age2         Age3

#>    [2,] 0.0065094031 0.25212865 0.7413619475
```
